payload:
# Weather Forecast Service

Before implementing various web APIs, we need to create a context in which we can use these APIs.
We need an educational application that will encapsulate various APIs into one project.
Without such an application, our web APIs would be loosely hanging code snippets that are hard to relate to.
Therefore we'll design an application--the Weather Forecast Service (WFS)--by translating client needs to domain concepts, and the concepts to the code.
We chose the weather bec... [truncated, 5431 chars total]

Full WFS specification written to .ralph/agent/spec.md covering domain models (City, WeatherRecord, WeatherForecast, WeatherAlert), REST API (JWT auth, CRUD endpoints), GraphQL API, Atom feed, WebSocket alerts, GitHub webhooks, admin CMS, infrastructure (Docker/Postgres/Redis), project structure, edge cases, and acceptance test plan.

Spec is comprehensive and implementable. Minor notes: (1) Pick Strawberry over Graphene for GraphQL, (2) Add DJANGO_SUPERUSER env vars to compose.yaml, (3) Clarify Daphne as ASGI server for both HTTP+WS or use process manager, (4) requirements.txt path is correct given Dockerfile COPY+WORKDIR semantics. None blocking.

All spec features implemented: REST API (cities, weather records, forecasts, alerts, JWT auth, health), GraphQL (Strawberry), Atom feed, WebSocket alerts (Django Channels), GitHub webhooks, admin CMS, seed data, startup/healthcheck scripts, BDD tests. Docker build verified. compose.yaml fixed (redis service, superuser env vars, WORKDIR override).

V1: GraphQL mutations bypass validation (schema.py uses Model.objects.create() without serializer/model validation for 7-day limit, temp_high>=temp_low, expires_at>starts_at). V2: ForecastInput line 104 uses strawberry.auto in @strawberry.input (must be @strawberry_django.input or use explicit type like datetime.date). V3: WebSocket consumer missing pong response handler in receive() - only handles subscribe type. V4: compose.yaml depends_on lacks condition: service_healthy for postgres and redis.

Fixed all 4 spec violations: V1 GraphQL mutations use serializer validation, V2 ForecastInput uses explicit date type, V3 WebSocket handles ping/pong, V4 compose depends_on uses service_healthy conditions

All 4 spec violations confirmed fixed. Full re-verification passed: infrastructure, models, REST API, GraphQL validation, WebSocket ping/pong, compose healthchecks, admin CMS, Atom feed, seed data. LOOP_COMPLETE.

Weather Forecast Service fully implemented and verified. All functional requirements satisfied: REST API, GraphQL, WebSocket alerts, Atom feed, GitHub webhooks, admin CMS, JWT auth, seed data for 5 biggest cities. All spec violations fixed and re-verified.

## Reason
completed

- Duration: 33m 5s
